name: Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA)

forces: Reuse of coarse services ↔ coupling through shared contracts
x.structure: Business capabilities are exposed as reusable, contract-defined services, often integrated via an enterprise service bus (ESB) with shared schemas and governance.
antiPatterns: ESB as a smart-pipe god component
x.scale: Enterprise; predecessor and cousin of microservices.

def: An enterprise style that structures functionality as reusable, contract-defined services integrated across the organisation, classically via an enterprise service bus.
problem: Large enterprises need to reuse and integrate capabilities across many heterogeneous applications.
mechanism: Each business capability is published as a service with a formal contract (often SOAP/WSDL historically); an ESB handles routing, transformation, and orchestration. Governance defines shared schemas and policies.
limits: ESB becomes a central bottleneck / single point of failure
Coarse services can hide tight coupling
whenNot: Greenfield where microservices’ decentralised model fits better
myths: "SOA and microservices are the same" — microservices favour smart endpoints / dumb pipes and decentralised data; classic SOA centralises via the ESB
